text: While Norway announced its intention to recognize the State of Palestine in May 2024, Denmark's position has been more nuanced. On March 9, 2011, Mahmoud Abbas, President of the Palestinian National Authority, visited Denmark for the first time to discuss bilateral relations. During the visit, Danish Foreign Minister Lene Espersen emphasized the responsibility of both parties to restart peace negotiations and encouraged Abbas to return to the negotiating table. Espersen also announced an upgrade to the Palestinian delegation's status in Copenhagen, signaling support for Palestinian state-building. Earlier, in August 2011, the Social Democrats revealed their intention to recognize the State of Palestine if they won the next parliamentary election, stating that the Palestinians have a right to a state and that they would recognize it upon request. However, Denmark's position on an independent Palestinian state in the United Nations was still unknown at the time.

During the 18th and 19th centuries, the site of Beit Nehemia was part of the Palestinian Arab village of Bayt Nabala. The area extended from the foothills in the east, through the Lod Valley to the outskirts of Jaffa in the west. Approximately 20 villages were located in this area, with inhabitants having access to tens of thousands of hectares of prime agricultural land.

During Wallerian degeneration, Schwann cells play an active role in removing myelin debris, both by degrading their own myelin and phagocytosing extracellular myelin. The phagocytic role of Schwann cells has been investigated through electron microscopy of in vivo and in vitro degenerating nerve segments. MAC-2 (also known as galectin-3), a galactose-specific lectin, is a molecule whose expression is studied in Schwann cells to understand their phagocytic role. MAC-2 expression is observed in both macrophage-rich in vivo degenerating nerves and macrophage-scarce in vitro degenerating nerves. A strong positive correlation exists between MAC-2 expression levels and the extent of myelin destruction by phagocytosis. Deficient MAC-2 production is associated with a failure to efficiently remove myelin. Besides phagocytosis, Schwann cells also proliferate and express major histocompatibility complex (MHC) class II molecules.

The 18th Avenue station has two fare control areas, one full-time and one no longer staffed. Divorce rates have climbed in the European Union and the United States with increase in autonomous marriage rates. The lowest divorce rates in the world are in cultures with high rates of arranged marriages such as Amish culture of United States (1%), Hindus of India (3%), and Ultra-Orthodox Jews of Israel (7%). According to a 2012 study by Statistic Brain, 53.25% of marriages are arranged worldwide. The global divorce rate for arranged marriages was 6.3%, which could be an indicator for the success rate of arranged marriages. This has led scholars to ask if arranged marriages are more stable than autonomous marriages, and whether this stability matters. Others suggest that the low divorce rate may not reflect stability, rather it may reflect the difficulty in the divorce process and social ostracism to the individuals, who choose to live in a dysfunctional marriage rather than face the consequences of a divorce. Also, the perception of high divorce rates attributed to self-arranged marriages in the United States is being called into question.

Sheets of cirrus clouds commonly fan out from the eye walls of tropical cyclones. (The eye wall is the ring of storm clouds surrounding the eye of a tropical cyclone.) A large shield of cirrus and cirrostratus typically accompanies the high altitude outflowing winds of tropical cyclones, and these can make the underlying bands of rain—and sometimes even the eye—difficult to detect in satellite photographs.

Molecular endofullerenes consist of fullerene cages encapsulating small molecules. Structures such as the dihydrogen endofullerene H2@C60, the water endofullerene H2O@C60, the hydrogen fluoride endofullerene HF@C60, and the methane endofullerene CH4@C60 have been synthesized. The encapsulated molecules display unusual physical properties, behaving as confined quantum rotors with rotational and translational quantization. Compression of molecular endofullerenes like H2@C60 may lead to dissociation of the encapsulated molecules and reaction with the fullerene cage, resulting in endohedral fullerene adducts.

The University of New South Wales (UNSW), often in collaboration with NICTA, has been a significant contributor to the L4 microkernel family. UNSW developers implemented L4 on MIPS64 and Alpha platforms, using assembler and C for the former. These implementations were based on the Version 2 API, with Jochen Liedtke's original L4 retrospectively named "Version 2". After the release of L4Ka::Pistachio, the DiSy group at UNSW maintained the MIPS and Alpha architectures of the kernel. L4Ka::Pistachio achieved super-fast local IPC and is considered the fastest microkernel on supported processors in terms of the ping-pong metric. The UNSW group also developed Wombat, a highly portable version of Linux, with context-switching costs on XScale processors significantly lower than native Linux. Wombat runs on processors such as the MC68040.

Capacitor discharge stud welding differs from drawn arc stud welding, in that capacitor discharge welding does not require flux. The weld time is shorter, enabling the weld to bond with little oxidation and no need for heat concentration. It also allows for small-diameter studs to be welded to thin, lightweight materials. This process uses a direct-current arc from a capacitor. The weld time in this process is between 1 and 6 milliseconds. Capacitor discharge stud welding with the latest equipment can create a weld without burn-through showing on the opposite side of very thin metals. CD stud welding is often used for smaller diameter studs and pins, as well as on non-standard materials and for accuracy. Drawn arc stud welding is primarily used for structural purposes and larger diameter weld studs.

Microhyla laterite is only known from few localities in Udupi district and Konaje, both in the Karnataka state, at elevations of 50–80 m (160–260 ft) above sea level. Buffalo Bill State Park derives its name from Colonel William F. "Buffalo Bill" Cody, a celebrated figure of the American West, renowned as a showman, promoter, and developer. Cody, who earned his "Buffalo Bill" moniker hunting buffalo for the Kansas Pacific Railroad work crews, first came to the Shoshone Canyon area in the 1870s as a guide for a survey expedition. Over the next two decades, he guided hunting parties in the region and worked to bring irrigation and agricultural development into Park County. Cody founded the town of Cody in 1896. Some of the land now encompassed by the park was originally owned by Cody, who sold it to the federal government for the reservoir project. Buffalo Bill Dam was constructed on the Shoshone River and completed in 1910.

The theory of constructed emotion (TCE) offers an explanation for how emotions are actively created by the brain, rather than being innate reactions. TCE formulates the brain as a predictive organ whose primary computational goal is to minimize prediction error. Once prediction error is minimized, the brain categorizes sensory events based on past experiences, sensory input, previous learning, and language. The brain continually updates and constructs an internal model of the world, including emotion concepts. When the brain's internal model constructs an emotion concept, it influences perception and action. Networks such as the default mode network and control networks are hypothesized to be involved in the generation and tuning of this internal model.

Choosing Wellington as his base, Rhodes built premises in Te Aro and by 1841 had completed a wharf and warehouses. He directed the family's land interests from Wellington, living at The Grange in Wadestown. By 1843, Rhodes had bought out Cooper and Holt's share of the business.

In 1991, a collaborative project was undertaken by a number of federal agencies in cooperation with provincial and territorial governments to establish a common ecological framework for Canada. A second report published in 1999 established the 53 ecoprovinces of Canada in accordance with the requirements of the Commission for Environmental Cooperation (CEC).

Laws on drunkenness vary. Past solar activity may be recorded by various proxies, including carbon-14 and beryllium-10. These indicate lower solar activity during the Maunder Minimum. The scale of changes resulting in the production of carbon-14 in one cycle is small (about one percent of medium abundance) and can be taken into account when radiocarbon dating is used to determine the age of archaeological artifacts. The interpretation of the beryllium-10 and carbon-14 cosmogenic isotope abundance records stored in terrestrial reservoirs such as ice sheets and tree rings has been greatly aided by reconstructions of solar and heliospheric magnetic fields based on historic data on Geomagnetic storm activity, which bridge the time gap between the end of the usable cosmogenic isotope data and the start of modern spacecraft data.

Regions that are or were seismic active and with the presence of fault zones might indicate there are ore deposits. A case study in Nevada, US by Howald (2015) studied how seismic-induced fluids accumulate mineral deposits, namely sinter and gold, along spaces provided by a fault zone. Two separate seismic events were identified and dated by oxygen isotopic concentrations, followed by episodes of the upward hydrothermal fluid migrations through permeable normal fault zone. Mineralization started to take place when these hot silica-rich hydrothermal fluids met the cool meteoric water infiltrated along the fault zone until the convective flow system was shut down. In order to deposit minerals, seismic events that bring hydrothermal fluids are not the only dominant factor, the permeability of the fault zone also has to be sufficient for permitting fluid flows.

Tropical cyclones, intense circular storms originating over warm tropical oceans, feature a central eye surrounded by an eyewall, where the strongest winds and heaviest rainfall occur. High-altitude outflowing winds often accompany cirrus and cirrostratus clouds that fan out from the eyewall. These high altitude clouds can obscure underlying rainbands and make the detection of the cyclone's structure difficult in satellite imagery.

Following the assassination of Julius Caesar in 44 BC, a power vacuum emerged in Rome, leading to the formation of the Second Triumvirate in 43 BC. This alliance, formally established by the Lex Titia, comprised Octavian, Mark Antony, and Marcus Lepidus, granting them absolute governing authority for five years. The triumvirate was renewed in 37 BC for another five years. Octavian viewed Mark Antony as a major rival, especially after Antony's relationship with Cleopatra VII, the mother of Caesar's son Caesarion. Antony's marriage to Octavia Minor, Octavian's sister, was intended to solidify the triumvirate, but Antony abandoned her and moved to Alexandria, where he began a long-term romance with Cleopatra and assumed a role in relation to Caesarion. These actions, along with other perceived transgressions, were viewed negatively by Octavian and much of the Roman Senate.

Capacitor discharge (CD) stud welding differs from drawn arc stud welding primarily in its application, weld time, and the materials it's suited for. Unlike drawn arc stud welding, CD stud welding doesn't require flux or shielding gas. CD stud welding is a nearly instantaneous process, with weld times as short as 0.01 seconds, compared to the longer weld times of drawn arc stud welding. This rapid weld time minimizes heat buildup, reducing distortion, discoloration, and burning, making it ideal for applications where appearance is critical. CD stud welding is often used on thinner base metals, including low carbon and stainless steels, and can even weld small-diameter studs to materials as thin as 0.020" (0.5mm) with the latest equipment. It uses a rapid discharge of electrical energy from a capacitor to create an intense arc. Drawn arc stud welding is generally used for larger diameter fasteners and thicker base metals.

The European Union expressed its concern about the personal information management. In May 2019, Clyde Chambliss sponsored the Human Life Protection Act in Alabama, which bans nearly all abortions except when necessary to prevent a serious health risk to the mother. The act does not allow for exceptions in cases of rape or incest. Doctors who perform abortions could face a Class A felony charge, punishable by up to 99 years in prison. Chambliss defended the law by stating, "When God creates the miracle of life inside a woman's womb, it is not our place as human beings to extinguish that life."

Bipolar disorder (BD) is a heterogeneous disease typically characterized by alternating dysfunctional mood states, shifting between mania (BD-I) or hypomania (BD-II) and depression, further accompanied by cognitive impairments. The main characteristic of bipolar disorder lies in the shift into two states with opposing affective valence. Neuroimaging studies reveal disrupted functional connectivity within several brain networks, including the default mode network (DMN), which plays a critical role in the pathophysiology of BD, and the sensorimotor network. The frontoparietal network also exhibits common and distinct neural activities in BD. Disrupted recruitment of the salience network (SN), in addition to decreased frontoparietal network recruitment, contributes to cognitive dysregulation in BD. Bipolar disorder is characterized by a functional imbalance between hyperactive ventral/limbic areas and hypoactive dorsal/cognitive brain regions. Aberrant connectivity and dysregulation within these networks contribute to affective and cognitive symptoms.

The Community Development Program (CDP) faced significant criticism, leading to a Senate inquiry by the Finance and Public Administration References Committee. The inquiry was initiated on 22 March 2017, with a report due by 14 September 2017. The final report included 96 recommendations covering legislative and regulatory changes. The committee concluded that the CDP could not continue in its current form and advocated for a new program. This new program should move away from a centralised, top-down administration. Instead, the report suggested a model where local communities are empowered to make decisions. The report also criticised the current CDP's administrative burden and focus on compliance.

Polyvocality and code-switching play a major role in the lyric essay. Both techniques allow for the lyric essay to be either very personal or to take a more objective tone. An example of this is found in Claudia Rankine's Citizen: An American Lyric, which the book's publisher classifies as both poetry and creative nonfiction—and is often referred to as work of lyric essays. Rankine code switches between highly personal, diaristic language and formal, academic language—as well as a variety of other types of language.

He acquired deeds to nearly two million acres. For the trading stations he placed agents and stock-in-trade at carefully chosen landings. For his own base he chose Wellington and in late 1840 built his premises in Te Aro and completed his own wharf, Wellington's first, the following year. For almost 50 years molecular biology was based on two dogmas: (i) equating biological function of the protein with a unique three-dimensional structure and (ii) assuming exquisite specificity in protein complexes. Specificity/selectivity is ensured by unambiguous set of interactions formed between the protein and its ligand (another protein, DNA, RNA or small molecule). Many protein complexes however, contain functionally important/critical regions, which remain highly dynamic in the complex or adopt different conformations. This phenomenon is defined fuzziness. The most pertinent example is the cyclin-dependent kinase inhibitor Sic1, which binds to the SCF subunit of Cdc4 in a phosphorylation dependent manner. No regular secondary structures are gained upon phosphorylation and the different phosphorylation sites interchange in the complex.

Research on sex differences in memory reveals nuanced patterns rather than a consistent female or male advantage. Women tend to retain stronger, more vivid memories of emotional events, recalling them more quickly and with greater richness and intensity. They also often outperform men in episodic memory tasks and recognition memory, potentially due to differences in scanning behavior. In spatial navigation, women tend to adopt an egocentric, landmark-based strategy, while men often employ an allocentric, Euclidean approach. Men, on average, exhibit superior visuospatial skills and working memory capacity. Estradiol, a primary form of estrogen, plays a significant role in women's memory performance and brain circuitry, with its depletion during menopause potentially impacting cognitive function.

The concept for greenmuseum.org emerged in the late 1990s from the collaborative sculpture group Meadowsweet Dairy, which included Henry Corning, Glenda Griffith, Dan Ustin, Alan Leavitt, and Alex Tereshkin. The artists recognized a need to address environmental issues through art. After meetings with artists, environmentalists, and technology experts, the name greenmuseum.org was chosen, and the collective began the process of becoming a non-profit, applying for federal tax-exempt status. The Herndon Home Museum, a National Historic Landmark, is located at 587 University Place NW in Atlanta, atop Diamond Hill, one of the city's highest points. The interior incorporates wood, stone, and plaster materials, and the first-floor walls feature hand-painted murals depicting scenes from the Herndon family history.

Microhomology-mediated end joining (MMEJ), also known as Ku-independent alternative nonhomologous end joining, is a pathway for double-strand break (DSB) repair involving at least five steps. Resembling homology recombination (HR), the first step involves end resection. Following this, microhomologous regions anneal, forming an intermediate with 3'-flaps and gaps. The heterologous flaps are then removed, followed by fill-in synthesis and ligation.

Accelerated soil burial tests are used to evaluate the biodegradability of materials, often textiles and biocomposites, by simulating long-term exposure to soil environments. These tests are performed to predict degradation behavior within a compressed timeframe relative to the material's intended service life. Samples are buried in biologically active soil, with high relative humidity, at a controlled temperature, often around 29°C, for periods ranging from weeks to months, sometimes up to 84 days or even 12 months. The soil's activity is confirmed using a reference material, such as cotton, that should degrade within a known timeframe. The experimental conditions must reflect a microbially active soil environment.

Schwann cells are active in Wallerian degeneration. They not only have a role in phagocytosis of myelin, but they also have a role in recruitment of macrophages to continue the phagocytosis of myelin. The phagocytic role of Schwann cells has been investigated by studying the expression of molecules in Schwann cells that are typically specific to inflammatory macrophages. Expression of one such molecule MAC-2, a galactose-specific lectin, is observed in not only degenerating nerves that are macrophage-rich but also degenerating nerves that are macrophage-scarce and Schwann cell-rich. Furthermore, the effects of MAC-2 in degenerating nerves are associated with myelin phagocytosis. There was a positive correlation between the amount of MAC-2 expression and the extent of myelin phagocytosis. A deficiency in MAC-2 expression can even cause inhibition of myelin removal from injury sites.

The European Union has expressed concerns regarding the protection of its citizens' personal data and privacy. In 2012, Viviane Reding, then a European Commissioner who previously served as Commissioner for Justice, Fundamental Rights, and Citizenship, spearheaded the proposal for the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR). The GDPR, which replaced Directive 95/46/EC, grants users the "right to be forgotten," allowing them to request data controllers, typically businesses, to erase their personal data. This right aims to give individuals control over their online information, encompassing the removal of facts and habits that reveal someone's lifestyle.

The University of Illinois Willard Airport (CMI) was dedicated on October 26, 1945, and named after former university president Arthur Cutts Willard. Scheduled airline service began in 1954, and the original terminal building was constructed in 1960. By 1969, Willard was the second-busiest airport in Illinois. However, the Airline Deregulation Act of 1978, which incentivized airlines to focus on larger hubs, led to a decline in service. The price differential to airfares from major hub airports such as Chicago O'Hare grew, and demand for tickets from Willard fell. The present terminal was completed in 1987.

The military order of Saint Lazarus of Jerusalem originated in a leper hospital founded in the twelfth century by crusaders of the Latin Kingdom. There had been earlier leper hospitals in the East, of which the Knights of St. Lazarus claimed to be the continuation, in order to have the appearance of remote antiquity and to pass as the oldest of all orders. According to Charles Moeller, "this pretension is apocryphal"; but documentary evidence does confirm that the edifice was a functioning concern in 1073.

The rejection of the U-Bahn in 1973 had significant consequences for Zurich's transport planning. Prior to this, the Social Democrats had submitted a popular initiative for a 200 million franc loan to extend the public transport system, specifically mentioning the tram network. This loan was to be payable over 25 years. However, on 13 March 1977, the initiative was defeated, despite receiving 93,478 votes, representing 46.3% of the vote. Despite this setback, some construction related to the U-Bahn had already commenced, including a tunnel section below the A1L motorway that was nearing completion. Of the approved 200 million francs, 82 million was earmarked for a new tramline from Milchbrück through the tunnel to Schwamendingen. This plan was later approved in a municipal referendum on 24 September 1978. The Milchbuck–Schwamendingen tunnel, a relic of the U-Bahn project, eventually opened to tram traffic on 1 February 1986, with lines 7 and 9 utilizing the tunnel and stopping at three underground stations: Schawamendingerplatz, Hirzenwiesen and Waldgarten. The tunnel is 3.6 km long.

The results from research on sex differences in memory are mixed and inconsistent, as some studies show no difference, and others show a female or male advantage. Females tend to perform better in episodic memory tasks and access their memories faster than males and use more emotional terms when describing memories. Females also outperform men in random word recall, semantic memory and autobiographical memory. Men are more likely to get the gist of events rather than be aware of specific details. Men also recall more factual information like childhood memories better than females, and also have increased spatial based memories. Men use strategies where they use mental spatial maps and are better at knowing absolute direction, like north and south. Following the Finnish government's ban of the Nordic Resistance Movement (NRM) in 2019, accelerationist and occultist-leaning members of the underground group were temporarily active as Atomwaffen Division Finland "Siitoin Squadron" (AWDSS). *French Kiss* was released in the United States on May 5, 1995, and received mixed reviews. In his review for the *San Francisco Chronicle*, Mick LaSalle wrote that director Lawrence Kasdan "takes what could have been a fluffy comedy with lots of plot complications and picturesque scenery and instead puts his focus on the important things: on the characters played by Ryan and Kline and how they happen to be feeling." Gerda Maria Ahlm was born in Vasteras in 1869, the daughter of Augusta Ebba and Lars Vilhelm Ahlm. From 1889 to 1891, she studied at the Royal Swedish Academy of Fine Arts. In 1896, she attended the Tallbergska course, where she studied etching. Ahlm travelled extensively throughout Europe for further study in 1894-1895, visiting France, Italy, and England.

Now located in Rettigska house at Villa Street 3 in Stockholm, the Academy had origins in the early 1700s Uppsala. It was founded in 1753 by Queen Louisa Ulrica, Queen of Sweden and the mother of King Gustav III and originally dedicated to literature. In 1786 when the Swedish Academy was founded it was reconstituted under its present name with new objectives, mainly dedicated to historical and antiquarian preservation. This included a close cooperation with the Swedish National Heritage Board (Swedish: "Riksantikvarieämbetet") whose director was, ex officio, the Academy's secretary.

In 2005, the Siuzdak Lab created an open-source tool named XCMS in the programming language R. Noticing the need for a more accessible, graphical data processing tool they created the cloud-based XCMS Online in 2012. The ability for users to stream data directly from instruments while being acquired was added in 2014. Also in that year a commercial version named XCMS Plus (owned by Mass Consortium Corporation) was released and, in 2015, SCIEX became a reseller. In 2017 it was shown that XCMS Online could be used in a systems biology workflow. One year later, in the absence of a publicly available alternative, a version of XCMS Online was released with the ability to perform multiple reaction monitoring (MRM).

Since its establishment in 1992, the 'Grodzka Gate – NN Theatre Centre' has been actively involved in documenting and preserving the history of Lublin, particularly its Jewish heritage, through its "Oral History" ("Historia Mówiona") project. This project centers around collecting testimonies from individuals who remember pre-World War II Lublin. The Centre has recorded over 2,500 hours of audio and video materials from more than 800 people, focusing on life in Lublin and the surrounding areas before the war. The 'Grodzka Gate' is among the ten largest digital libraries in Poland, and its digital resources are available online. The project uses the dLibra system, ensuring compliance with international projects like Europeana.

Fault zones, particularly those exhibiting fractures and crushed rock, can act as conduits for hydrothermal solutions, potentially leading to the formation of ore deposits. A 2015 case study by Howald in Nevada focused on the role of seismic activity in the formation of epithermal gold and silver deposits. The study identified and dated two separate seismic events using felt-report surveys and seismogram analysis. This was followed by an examination of the relationship between these events and the timing of mineralization. The study concluded that seismic activity likely triggered the release of mineral-rich fluids into the fault zone, initiating mineralization. The deposition of minerals requires a combination of factors, including the presence of hydrothermal solutions, suitable host rocks, and favorable geochemical conditions within the fault zone.

Every night, all human dreams are played out in Nightopia and Nightmare, the two parts of the dream world. In Nightopia, distinct aspects of dreamers' personalities are represented by luminous coloured spheres known as "Ideya". The evil ruler of Nightmare, Wizeman the Wicked, is stealing this dream energy from sleeping visitors in order to gather power and take control of Nightopia and eventually the real world. To achieve this, he creates five beings called "Nightmaren": jester-like, flight-capable beings, which include Jackle, Clawz, Gulpo, Gillwing and Puffy as well as many minor maren. He also creates two "Level One" Nightmaren: Nights and Reala. However, Nights rebels against Wizeman's plans, and is punished by being imprisoned inside an Ideya palace, a container for dreamers' Ideya.

In the General Certificate of Secondary Education (GCSE) exam taken by secondary school students in England and Wales, grades generally range from 9 (highest) to 1 (lowest). These replaced earlier A, B, C... grading. However, in GCSE Science, Mathematics Statistics, and any Modern Foreign Language, there are two tiers (higher and foundation). In the higher tier, grades 9 to 4 can be achieved, while in the foundation tier, only grades 5 to 1 can be awarded. The new 9–1 qualifications saw some more subjects such as English language and English literature go 'tierless,’ with the same paper covering all levels of demand. Generally, a 4 or above would be considered a pass and a 3 or below would be considered a fail by most institutions: for Mathematics and English Language and English Literature, and possibly Science, this would require a resit.

The only known host plant for B. steniellus is the sugarcane. The female moth lays a batch of 100 to 300 eggs on the midrib of a sugarcane leaf. The larvae hatch out after about a week and make their way into the stem by drilling holes just above a node. They excavate galleries inside the stem, feeding voraciously. When the cane dries up and the crown of leaves dies in about ten days, the larvae move to a different cane in the vicinity. They feed for three to four weeks, passing through five instar stages, before pupating inside a stem. The adult moths emerge in six to twelve days. The male/female ratio varies between forty and sixty percent in the different generations. The whole life cycle lasts about five or six weeks, and there may be two or three generations in each year.

As a Commonwealth realm, Tuvalu recognizes the monarch as its head of state. Traditionally, the sovereign was seen as the font of justice, with courts operating under royal authority. However, in Tuvalu, criminal offences are defined by national legislation, including laws against transnational organized crime and IUU fishing. Proceedings for indictable offences are brought in the name of the King (Rex). While common law held that "the King could do no wrong," and the monarch is typically immune from prosecution in their own courts, this does not apply internationally.

In 1950, the U.S. Congress authorized Puerto Rico to draft its own constitution through Pub.L. 81-600, aiming to establish a constitutional government. The Constitutional Assembly met between 1951 and 1952. Framers had to establish a republican government and include a Bill of Rights.

Window air conditioners and packaged terminal air conditioners (PTAC) are similar "through-the-wall" units. PTACs, often found in hotels and apartment buildings, can provide both cooling and heating. In cold climates, heat pumps, including geothermal and cold-climate air source heat pumps, can be adapted to provide heating. PTACs can utilize a building's hot water system or reverse the refrigerant flow to provide heat. PTACs are installed in an exterior-facing wall, using a wall sleeve. A custom grill, color-matched to the building's specifications, covers the exterior opening. Window air conditioners can be installed in a window.

The military order of Saint Lazarus of Jerusalem originated in a leper hospital founded in the twelfth century by crusaders of the Latin Kingdom. As of the 2000 census, there were 22,380 people residing in Randolph County, organized into 8,742 households and 6,340 families. The population density was 34.4 people per square mile. *Allobates femoralis* inhabits the Amazon Basin and tropical eastern South America. Its range includes Guyana, Suriname, French Guiana, and Brazil, as well as the eastern parts of Colombia, Ecuador, Peru, and Bolivia. A single reported sighting in Venezuela may have referred to *Ameerega picta*. This diurnal frog is typically found on the forest floor in lowland rainforests, usually below 300 m, but can occur up to 1,000 m in Colombia and Ecuador.

Luca Pacioli, sometimes called Lucas di Burgo, was born in Borgo San Sepolcro (now Borgo Sansepolcro) in Tuscany, likely around 1445, though some sources suggest a later date as late as 1450. He was the son of Bartolomeo Pacioli and is said to have lived with the Befolci family as a child. In Sansepolcro, Pacioli received an *abbaco* education, which focused on practical knowledge for merchants and was taught in the local language rather than Latin. Around 1464, he moved to Venice, where he tutored the sons of a merchant while continuing his education and developing significant knowledge in commercial accounting. It was during this period that he wrote his first arithmetic treatise. Pacioli was a Franciscan friar between 1472 and 1475.

Closed fullerenes encapsulating small molecules have been synthesized. Representative are the synthesis of the dihydrogen endofullerene H2@C60, the water endofullerene H2O@C60, the hydrogen fluoride endofullerene HF@C60, and the methane endofullerene CH4@C60. The encapsulated molecules display unusual physical properties which have been studied by a variety of physical methods. As shown theoretically, compression of molecular endofullerenes (e.g., H2@C60) may lead to dissociation of the encapsulated molecules and reaction of their fragments with interiors of the fullerene cage. Such reactions should result in endohedral fullerene adducts, which are currently unknown.

The initial reception to *House of Usher* was mixed. While a review appeared in *The New York Times* on September 15, 1960, it was not written by Eugene Archer. St. Helena Parish contains the only identified meteorite impact crater in the state of Louisiana. This suspected impact crater is a roughly circular depression about 1.2 miles/2 km in diameter. Shocked quartz and intensely fractured quartz have been recovered from fractured and possibly altered sediments comprising its rim. Its age is estimated to be between 11 and 30 ka. It lies about 5.8 miles/9.3 kilometers southwest of Greensburg, in the southwest corner of the parish. Louisiana Highway 37 cuts through the northern edge of this feature.

The theory of constructed emotion (TCE) offers an explanation of the basis of emotion by formulating the brain as a running internal model that controls central pattern generators in order to maintain allostasis. This theory argues that the computational goal of the brain is to minimize the prediction error, unpredicted events, that arise in a particular sensory environment. Once prediction error is minimized, the brain's predictions become experiences and perceptions and the brain categorizes sensory events. In this manner, the brain continually updates and constructs its categorizations and predictions. When the brain's internal model constructs an emotion concept, the subsequent categorization elicits an emotion. It is hypothesized that the brain's default mode network is necessary to the generation its internal model while the salience network tunes the internal model by minimizing prediction error.

Football (soccer) is the most popular sport in Ta'if, as it is throughout Saudi Arabia. Wej Sports Club (Wej SC), which plays in the Saudi Second Division, represents the city. Impound races were first implemented in NASCAR in 2005 as a way to discourage teams from focusing solely on qualifying setups and instead prioritize race setups. NASCAR officials hoped this would enhance overall competition by encouraging teams to arrive at the track with the best possible race setup. In 2005, 21 of the 36 NASCAR Nextel Cup Series races were run under the impound rule. John Darby, then NASCAR Nextel Cup Series director, believed the rule would improve competition. However, the initial implementation caused confusion and wasted time for teams. After criticism from the garage, NASCAR reduced the impound race schedule to five races in 2006. NASCAR president Mike Helton stated that the rule "didn't fit the way we thought it would" and that they needed to "take a look at it from a different angle" to find the right balance. The impound rules are stated in NASCAR's rulebook.

Numerous incidents of public disorder occurred from the start of the uprising. On January 28 the police withdrew from the streets of Egypt following break-ins to a number of prisons. Prison guards allegedly allowed and aided the escape of inmates. Microhomology-mediated end joining (MMEJ), also known as alt-non-homologous end joining, is another pathway to repair DSBs. The process of MMEJ can be summarized in five steps: the 5' to 3' cutting of DNA ends, annealing of microhomology, removing heterologous flaps, and ligation and synthesis of gap filling DNA. It was found that the selection between MMEJ and NHEJ is mainly dependent on Ku levels and the concurrent cell cycle.

This station has two fare control areas, one at each end of the platforms. The full-time side is at the east (railroad south) end. A single staircase from each platform goes up to a metal crossover, where a short staircase goes up to a set of doors that lead to the station house's waiting area. A turnstile bank provides access to and from the station. Outside fare control, there is a token booth and doors leading to the west side of 18th Avenue between 63rd and 64th Streets. Development also occurred at the University of New South Wales (UNSW), where developers implemented L4 on several 64-bit platforms. Their work resulted in L4/MIPS and L4/Alpha, resulting in Liedtke's original version being retrospectively named L4/x86. Like Liedtke's original kernels, the UNSW kernels (written in a mix of assembly and C) were unportable and each implemented from scratch. With the release of the highly portable L4Ka::Pistachio, the UNSW group abandoned their own kernels in favor of producing highly tuned ports of L4Ka::Pistachio, including the fastest-ever reported implementation of message passing (36 cycles on the Itanium architecture). The group has also demonstrated that device drivers can perform equally well at user-level as in-kernel, and developed Wombat, a highly portable version of Linux on L4 that runs on x86, ARM, and MIPS processors. On XScale processors, Wombat context-switching costs are up to 50 times lower than in native Linux.

*Hierodoris tygris* is endemic to New Zealand. Within New Zealand, it has been found in Alfredton and Herepai. The type locality is not specified in the provided contexts. However, specimens have been collected in Manurewa and in the Wellington region.

Luca Pacioli was born between 1446 and 1448 in the Tuscan town of Sansepolcro where he received an abbaco education. This was education in the vernacular (i.e., the local tongue) rather than Latin and focused on the knowledge required of merchants. His father was Bartolomeo Pacioli; however, Luca Pacioli was said to have lived with the Befolci family as a child in his birth town Sansepolcro. He moved to Venice around 1464, where he continued his own education while working as a tutor to the three sons of a merchant. It was during this period that he wrote his first book, a treatise on arithmetic for the boys he was tutoring. Between 1472 and 1475, he became a Franciscan friar. Thus, he could be referred to as Fra ('Friar') Luca.

The 2016 United States presidential election, held every four years, determined the nation's next President and Vice President. To be eligible for these offices, Article Two of the U.S. Constitution mandates that candidates must be natural-born citizens, at least 35 years old, and have resided in the United States for a minimum of 14 years. Individuals typically seek the nomination of a political party, such as the Democratic or Republican Party, to become a presidential candidate. Parties generally select their nominee through a process involving state-level presidential preference primaries or caucuses, culminating in a formal nomination at a national nominating convention where state delegates cast their votes. Primary elections are when voters decide a party's candidate for the upcoming general election. In November, voters across the country cast ballots for their preferred presidential candidate and their running mate.

Rafael Brache married Altagracia (Grace) Brache Bernard, also Dominican. Together, they had five children: Rafael, Robert (Tico), Jose Guillermo, Susana (Sisi), and Thomas Edward Perez Brache. Their son, Rafael Jr., was involved in the Cayo Confites expedition, an attempt to overthrow Dominican Republic dictator Rafael Trujillo, around 1947. While the family lived in Puerto Rico for a time, most of the children ultimately settled in Buffalo, New York. One of Rafael Brache's grandsons is Thomas Edward (Tom) Perez, who served as Secretary of Labor under President Barack Obama. In 2017, Tom Perez was elected chair of the Democratic National Committee, becoming the first Latino to hold that post. A July 2016 *Wall Street Journal* article questioned Perez's representation of his grandfather's connections to Trujillo's regime, suggesting a more complex relationship than Perez portrayed.

Vicente Greco, nicknamed "Garrote," was born in Buenos Aires, Argentina, on February 3, 1888, to Italian immigrants Genaro and Victoria Greco. His father played the mandolin, and his siblings were passionate about music and learning. The family was very poor, forcing Vicente to interrupt his elementary school studies and sell newspapers on the streets of Buenos Aires from a young age. Greco was a self-taught musician who initially relied on fellow musicians to write down his compositions.

Robert Fordham entered politics with an unsuccessful run for the electorate of Syndal. In the 1970 Victorian election, he was elected as a member for the electorate of Reservoir. In 1970, Fordham was appointed Shadow Minister for Social Welfare and Aboriginal Affairs. He later became deputy leader of the Labor Party in Victoria, serving as deputy opposition leader under the leadership of Frank Wilkes.

The great spotted woodpecker is hunted by woodland birds of prey such as the Eurasian sparrowhawk and the northern goshawk. It is a host of the blood-feeding fly *Carnus hemapterus*, and its internal parasites may include the spiny-headed worm *Prosthorhynchus transversus*. The protozoan *Toxoplasma gondii*, which causes toxoplasmosis, can be potentially fatal. The great spotted woodpecker is also the favoured host of the tapeworm *Anomotaenia brevis*.

Pinchback and his wife Nina were the maternal grandparents of Jean Toomer. Their daughter Nina Pinchback Toomer returned to live with her parents after her husband abandoned her when Jean was an infant. They helped raise him, and he started school in Washington, D.C.. After his mother remarried, they moved to New Rochelle, New York. He returned to his grandparents after his mother died in 1909, and went to high school at the academic M Street School. As an adult, Toomer became a poet and writer who was prominent as a modernist in New York during the Harlem Renaissance.

In November 2013, Gurbaksh Singh Khalsa began a 44-day hunger strike to raise awareness about Sikh political prisoners. Isaac dated actor Patrice Robitaille from 2003 to 2011. In 2006, she gave birth to a daughter, named Lili-Alacie. She also has a son, born in 2014. In February 2018, she revealed that she was pregnant with her third child, a boy.

Mr. Happypants is a voodoo puppet character featured in Brian Brushwood's stage shows and podcasts. Brushwood initially chose the cute name ironically, intending to replace it later, envisioning a mean character. However, Mr. Happypants evolved into a purely evil persona, speaking through Brushwood with the aid of a voice distorter. In 1974, The Waiters performed their first annual "Spring Fever" concert. The Waiters performed this show annually until 2016. In the early 1990s, the Waiters added a December Holiday Show to their yearly concert repertoire. The Holiday Show was performed every December through the 2000s.

Bellwood is a village located in Cook County, Illinois. The most recent census in 2020 recorded a population of 18,716. In 1985, Simon Baron-Cohen, Alan M. Leslie, and Uta Frith suggested that autistic children do not understand that others have their own unique mental states, experiences, and beliefs. This seminal work posited that autistic individuals lack a "theory of mind" (ToM). While language skills can influence performance on ToM tasks, some difficulties persist even when verbal abilities are matched (Casten et al., 2022). This perceived ToM deficit has been routinely portrayed as an inseparable part of autism. However, Gernsbacher and Yergeau (2019) argued that the claim that autistic people lack a ToM is empirically questionable, and that the original studies have failed to replicate. A meta-analysis of replications of classic theory of mind studies yielded a small but statistically significant effect size of 0.15 (p = 0.01).

Woodland birds of prey such as the Eurasian sparrowhawk and the northern goshawk hunt the great spotted woodpecker. When the American Civil War formally began in April 1861 with the Confederate attack on Fort Sumter, Robert F. R. Lewis was aboard the USS *Sabine*. The town of Saint-Narcisse was named in honour of Saint Narcissus, whose statue sits atop the main altar of the Saint-Narcisse church. Football in Turkey traces its origins to the late 19th century, when Englishmen introduced the sport while residing in Salonica, then part of the Ottoman Empire. The inaugural league competition was the Istanbul Football League, commencing in the 1904-05 season. Several regional leagues emerged between the establishment of the Istanbul League and the creation of the Millî Lig (Süper Lig) in 1959, including those in Adana, Ankara, Eskisehir, Izmir, Bursa, and Trabzon. Prior to the Süper Lig, the Turkish Football Championship served as the first competition to determine a national champion, running from 1924 to 1951. This championship operated as a knockout competition, featuring the winners of the country's top regional leagues. The National Division (Millî Küme), the first national league competition in Turkey, began in 1937 and lasted until 1950, comprising the strongest clubs from the Ankara, Istanbul, and Izmir leagues.

Pinipig are characteristically light green when fresh, but usually become yellowish-white to brown when toasted. They superficially resemble grains of oats and are often confused with puffed rice. The texture is crunchy on the exterior with a chewy center.

Daud Haider is a Bangladeshi poet known for his freethoughts and writings. He served as the literary editor of the well-known Dhaka daily, *Dainik Sambad*, the largest circulated Bengali daily in North-East India. His poem "Kalo Surjer Kalo Joshnay Kalo Bonnay," published in 1974, which depicted figures from different religions, sparked controversy and accusations of blasphemy. Within the Commonwealth realms, the sovereign is responsible for rendering justice for all his subjects, and is thus traditionally deemed the fount of justice. In Tuvalu, criminal offences are legally deemed to be offences against the sovereign and proceedings for indictable offences are brought in the sovereign's name in the form of Rex versus [Name] ("Rex" being Latin for "King"). Hence, the common law holds that the sovereign "can do no wrong"; the monarch cannot be prosecuted in his or her own courts for criminal offences.

During the 1947-48 Kashmir Operations Flt. Lieutenant C D Subbaiah was distinguished for the operational missions flown during the war. This contributed to the successful capture of Gurais by the Indian Army. For this he was awarded the Vir Chakra later on 26 January 1950, the first Republic Day.

Infant mortality rates among refugee populations in the United States present a complex picture. Research using data from 1995 to 2000 found that infants born to Mexican immigrant women in the U.S. experienced mortality rates approximately 10% lower than those of non-Hispanic white women, supporting the "epidemiologic paradox." Despite facing socioeconomic challenges, some refugee communities, particularly those from Latin America, exhibit high fertility rates alongside low infant and childhood mortality. In contrast, Palestine refugee newborns in Gaza have experienced increased mortality rates, potentially due to inadequate neonatal care. The leading causes of infant and neonatal deaths among Palestine refugees are similar to those in other regions with limited resources.

Augustus Abbott was born in London on January 7, 1804. He was the eldest son of Henry Alexius Abbott, a retired Calcutta merchant, and Margaret (née Welsh), daughter of John Welsh of Edinburgh. Starkville was founded in 1832 as the county seat of Lee county. By 1837 a courthouse had been erected and by 1840 a jail was built. Cephaloridine is a cephalosporin compound with pyridinium-1-ylmethyl and 2-thienylacetamido side groups. The molecular nucleus of all cephalosporins is A3-7-aminocephalosporanic acid. Conformations around the β-lactam rings are similar to penicillin, while those at the carboxyl group exocyclic to the dihydrothiazine and thiazolidine rings differ.

The Pteroclidae was formerly included in the Galliformes due to the similarities the family shares with the true grouse. However, it was later discovered that these similarities are superficial and a result of convergent evolution. Sandgrouse were later placed near the Columbiformes largely due to their reported ability to drink by the "sucking" or "pumping" action of peristalsis of the esophagus, an unusual characteristic. More recently, it has been reported that they cannot suck up water in this way, and they are now treated separately in the order Pterocliformes. They have been considered near passerine birds, and are thought by some to be closer to the shorebirds (Charadriiformes).

A proponent of foreign trade, Barack Obama believed such agreements could drive America's economic growth. In October 2011, the U.S. entered into free trade agreements with South Korea, Colombia, and Panama, originally negotiated by the Bush administration. Obama renegotiated these agreements before submitting them to Congress. While Republicans overwhelmingly supported the deals, many Democrats opposed them, fearing job losses. Obama also aimed to conclude the Doha Round to lower global trade barriers.

Andrea Debono was born in Isla, Malta, on November 7, 1821, to Captain Michael Debono, a merchant navy captain, and Teresa nee Carabott. He had a twin sister named Battistina. Debono studied chemistry and medicine during his youth.

*Microhyla laterite*, also known as the Laterite narrow-mouthed frog, is a small species, with adult males measuring 15.3-16.6 mm in snout–vent length. The single known female measured 18.4 mm. The overall colouration is pale brown with prominent black markings on its dorsum, hands, feet, and flanks. A prominent black line rimmed with red runs across the back between the forelimbs. The vocal sac is dark purplish black when calling. The iris is golden in colour. The ventral parts, excluding the throat, are creme white without marbling.

Traffic control for automated guided vehicles (AGVs) within a facility can be achieved through centralized systems or local methods. In November 2013, Khalsa went on a 44-day hunger strike. The campaign was ended after Giani Gurbachan Singh (Akal Thakat Jathedar), said to take up the issue with the State Government. He was born in Buenos Aires, the son of Genaro Greco and Victoria Santo, a couple of modest means who brought up Vicente and seven other children. Gaul played college soccer for Bradley University from 2008 to 2011. In 2011, he was named NSCAA All-Midwest Region and All-Missouri Valley Conference. In 2010, Gaul was named All-Missouri Valley Conference as a forward, after converting from a midfielder.

In May 1947 the school board granted permission for a concert by Paul Robeson in the auditorium that was primarily to consist of traditional African American spirituals. CNG auto rickshaws are very popular mode of transport for short routes within the city. Accelerated soil burial tests are used to record the degradation process of the plastic in the ground by replicating the conditions of a landfill, a typical disposal site for plastics. These tests are used after the service life of the material has been depleted, and the next step for the material is disposal. Typically, samples are buried in biologically active soil for six months and are exposed to air to ensure that there is sufficient oxygen so that the aerobic mechanism of degradation can occur. The experimental conditions must reflect natural conditions closely, so the moisture and temperature of the soil are carefully controlled. The type of soil used must also be recorded, as it can affect the degradation process.

Ruddy attended Xavier High School, Holy Cross College (A.B., 1959), served in the USMC, and received his Ph.D. in International Law from Cambridge University (1969). He also received a LL.B. in 1965 from Loyola University and a LL.M. in 1967 from New York University.

In 1995 Manfred Feist suffered a stroke, after which he was paralysed down one side of his body. Following a long period of ill-health he died in Berlin on 17 December 2012. Born in Berlin-Charlottenburg, Hülper received vocal training at the Berlin University of the Arts. Afterwards she attended the acting school of the Oldenburgisches Staatstheater. *Asclepias incarnata*, commonly known as swamp milkweed, is an upright, clump-forming perennial that typically grows to be 100 to 150 cm (39 to 59 inches) tall. It emerges in late spring and has branched stems. Swamp milkweed grows from knotty roots about 1 inch in diameter. Its smooth, narrow leaves are lance-shaped with sharp tips and can be up to 15 cm (6 inches) long. Sometimes the leaf's edges turn inward and upward.

Interstate 229 (I-229) begins at a system interchange with I-29 and US 71 in Buchanan County, Missouri. As a sophomore, Lavoy Allen increased his scoring average to 10.9 points per game, the second-highest on the team. The relationship between abnormal feeding patterns and language patterns and language performance on the BSID-III at 18–22 months among extremely premature infants was evaluated. 1477 preterm infants born at <26 weeks gestation completed an 18-month neurodevelopmental follow-up assessment including the Receptive and Expressive Language Subscales of the BSID-III. It was determined with the help of the BSID-III that at 18 months adjusted age, premature infants with a history of feeding difficulties are more likely to have a language delay.

During the 1970s and 1980s he was invited to decorate and furnish several important places for important people. For many years, molecular biology was based on two central dogmas. The first, proposed by Francis Crick, describes the flow of biochemical information from DNA to RNA to proteins. This dogma emphasizes the residue-by-residue transfer of sequential information, clarifying that information cannot be transferred back from protein to either protein or nucleic acid. Specificity and selectivity are crucial in protein complex formation. However, many protein complexes contain regions that remain highly dynamic or adopt different conformations. This phenomenon, where functionally important regions exhibit such behavior, is called fuzziness. A pertinent example of fuzziness is the cyclin-dependent kinase inhibitor Sic1, which binds to the CDK phospho-adaptor subunit Cks1 in a phosphorylation-dependent manner.

Uddapanda Harshika Poonacha was born in Ammathi, Kodagu, Karnataka, and is the only daughter of her parents. An engineering graduate, she is married to Bhuvan Ponnanna, an actor and former Bigg Boss Kannada contestant. Lithin is her cousin.
